sections.title: I. Choose the word / phrase / sentence that best fits the space
II. Look at the sign or the notice. Choose the best answer.
III. Choose the word that best fits each space in the following passage.
sections.passageContent:
Dear Mom,

I'm having a fantastic school trip in Hue! Yesterday, our class went to visit some (17)___ ruins and I learned so much about our country's past. The most impressive part was the ancient (18)___ which was built hundreds of years ago. I was fascinated by the long history (19)___ this place, and our tour (20)___ told us many interesting stories about the kings who lived there.

Although some parts of the site were damaged by time, the government has started to (21)___ the old structures to keep them looking (22)___ for future generations.

It was a very peaceful experience, and I took many photos to show you. I feel very proud of our heritage and can't wait to share more details when I return home this Sunday!

sections.title: V. Read the following flyer and answer the questions.
sections.passageContent:
Gen Z's New Approach to Social Drinking

In Vietnam, social drinking has traditionally been a major part of life, especially during family holidays like Tet. However, a significant shift is happening. Generation Z-those born after 1997-is increasingly saying "no" to beer and liquor. They are pushing back against the social pressure from older relatives who often expect young people to join every toast. For many young people, drinking is no longer seen as a requirement for building relationships or having fun.

For instance, Thanh Tri, a 26-year-old consultant, decided to quit drinking after a heavy night left him unable to work for two days. He discovered that meeting partners in cafes is just as effective as meeting in bars. He believes that genuine connections are made through conversation, not alcohol.

Recent data supports this trend. A 2025 report showed that 45% of Vietnamese Gen Z respondents limit or avoid alcohol, compared to only 28% of Millennials. As a result, the market is changing. The production of non-alcoholic beer rose by 9% last year, while traditional beer sales declined. Local business owners also notice that young customers spend less time at beer shops and prefer ordering food over large amounts of alcohol.

Experts believe this change represents a new kind of maturity. In the past, drinking was a way to prove you were friendly or "part of the group." Today, being able to refuse a drink is seen as a sign of confidence and self-control. Young people now prioritize their health and mental clarity. While alcohol remains part of the culture, it is finally becoming a personal choice rather than a social obligation.

sections.title: VI. Use the correct form of the word given in each sentence.
VII. Look at the entry of the word 'agreement' in a dictionary.
sections.passageContent:
agreement /ə'gri:mənt/
noun
1 an arrangement, a promise or a contract made with somebody

They have entered into a free trade agreement with Australia.
The documents are covered by a confidentiality agreement.
The software is provided under a license agreement and may not be copied without permission.
The specific terms of the agreement have not been disclosed.

sections.title: VIII. Rewrite each of the following sentences...

questions.text: Which word has the underlined part pronounced differently from that of the others?
Which word has the underlined part pronounced differently from that of the others?
Which word has a different stress pattern from that of the others?
Which word has a different stress pattern from that of the others?

Scientist: The experiment didn't produce the results we wanted.
Assistant: ____ we have to rethink our whole strategy, unfortunately.

Nam: The city is ____ modern, but it's losing its traditional charm.
Ba: I agree. Many old houses have been replaced by skyscrapers.

Linh: Why is the shop closed today?
Mai: It has ____ because it couldn't compete with the big mall nearby.

Teacher: "Are you ____ for the final speaking test, Mai?"
Mai: "I've been practicing all morning, teacher."

Bình: "Are you worried ____ the air quality in the city?"
An: "Yes, the smog is getting worse."

Tom: "Have you seen the video of the dancing cat?"
Linh: "Yes! It went ____ and got millions of views in just one day."

Tom: Let's go to the Ben Thanh Market this weekend, ____?
Jerry: That's a great idea! I need to buy some souvenirs.

Tuấn: I met Mr. Vinh yesterday.
Khang: Is he the man ____ son is a famous football player?

Interviewer: "How do you handle working under high pressure and tight deadlines?"
Candidate: "____ In fact, I find that I am often most productive when I have a clear goal and a time limit."
options: I tend to thrive in fast-paced environments.
I'm not sure I can do that.

questions.text:
Son: "Dad, I'm thinking of dropping out of university to become a full-time YouTuber."
Father: "____ You should at least finish your degree first to have a backup plan."
options: I'm in two minds about this.
Don't you think that's a bit risky?

questions.text: What does the 'KEEP RIGHT' sign mean?
What does the 'NOTICE RESTROOMS THIS WAY PLEASE' sign mean?
options: The restrooms are in the opposite direction.
Follow the sign to find the restrooms.

questions.text: Question 17.
Question 18.
Question 19.
Question 20.
Question 21.
Question 22.
In the past, young people in Vietnam were often expected by their older relatives to join in every toast.
Thanh Tri decided to quit drinking because he found it too expensive to go to bars every night.
According to a 2025 report, Millennials are more likely to avoid alcohol than Gen Z respondents.
The sales of traditional beer decreased last year, while the production of non-alcoholic beer increased.
What does Thanh Tri believe is the best way to make "genuine connections" with partners?
options: By inviting them to a heavy night of drinking at a local bar.
By meeting them in cafes and focusing on real conversation.
By following the traditional social rules of older relatives.
By avoiding work for two days after a social event.

questions.text: According to the text, how has the meaning of "refusing a drink" changed in Vietnam?
options: It is now seen as a sign of being unfriendly or not part of the group.
It is considered a requirement for anyone born before 1997.
It is now viewed as a sign of confidence and having self-control.
It is a social obligation that every young person must follow during Tet.

questions.text: 29. She was ____ to start her new job. (excite)
30. The well-____ student aced the exam. (preparation)
31. They ____ offered refreshments to all their guests. (hospitable)
32. She ____ tried skydiving for the first time. (adventure)
33. The ____ park was full of rides and games. (amuse)
34. The ____ of having nothing to do made the minutes feel like hours. (bore)
35. The neighboring countries signed a ____ to sell products without paying extra taxes.
36. New employees must sign a ____ so they do not share any secret information.

37. She began working as a teacher in 2015.
→ She has ____

38. It's a pity that it's raining now.
→ I wish ____
questions.correctText: it would not be raining now, it were not raining now, it wasn't raining now

questions.text:
39. "Listen to me carefully," the teacher said to the students.
→ The teacher told the students ____

40. They are going to organize an environmental campaign.
→ An environmental campaign is going ____ (place)
